meta.description: Curated CLI Matrix registry for CLI-Hub multi-CLI workflows (capability-based, v2 schema)

matrices.name: video-creation
matrices.description: Capability-based matrix for end-to-end video production: storyboard planning, story/audio direction, internet video/music search/download with source triage, capture, generation, voice/music, sound design, footage analysis, transcription, high-end caption design, assembly, overlay, thumbnail, encode, and quality review with render-doctor investigation. Providers include harness CLIs, public CLIs, Python libs, native binaries, bundled scripts, agent skills, and cloud APIs.

capabilities.intent: Turn a brief into global creative direction, narrative/emotional arc, audio arc, script, shot list, timing map, storyboard, and asset requirements before production. Default to agent-native planning unless a focused external skill is clearly worth installing.

providers.name: Agent-native planning
providers.notes: Default path for creative_direction.md, story arc, emotional curve, audio arc, beat map, scripts, shot lists, timing maps, storyboards, and asset plans; read cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/story-structure-audio.md for non-trivial videos.

providers.name: storyboard-creation skill
providers.notes: Install with `pnpm dlx add-skill https://github.com/inference-sh/skills/tree/HEAD/guides/video/storyboard-creation`. Use only for shot grammar, camera angles, continuity, storyboard panels, and animatic planning.

providers.name: remotion-best-practices skill
providers.notes: Install with `npx skills add https://github.com/remotion-dev/skills --skill remotion-best-practices`; SKILL.md: https://github.com/remotion-dev/skills/blob/main/skills/remotion/SKILL.md. Use when the storyboard will become Remotion/React motion-video code.

capabilities.intent: Discover candidate internet footage, stock clips, public-domain clips, or platform URLs before download.

providers.name: Web search + source filters
providers.notes: Use site/source filters for YouTube, Bilibili, Wikimedia Commons, stock sites, and public-source searches; record URL, creator, license, and attribution before editing. For found-footage deliverables, read cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/source-triage.md and classify each candidate's platform-origin evidence.

capabilities.intent: Download or import selected web video URLs into the workspace with provenance metadata.

providers.name: yt-dlp
providers.install_hint: pipx install yt-dlp  # or brew install yt-dlp
providers.notes: General downloader for YouTube, Bilibili, and many sites; supports search, playlists, cookies, and quality filters.

providers.name: you-get
providers.install_hint: pipx install you-get  # or brew install you-get
providers.notes: Multi-site media downloader with Bilibili support.

providers.name: lux
providers.install_hint: brew install lux  # or go install github.com/iawia002/lux@latest
providers.notes: Go CLI downloader with Bilibili, YouTube, Douyin, Youku, and other site support.

providers.name: BBDown
providers.install_hint: dotnet tool install --global BBDown  # or brew install bbdown
providers.notes: Bilibili-focused CLI; useful for subtitles, danmaku, playlists, favorites, and high-quality streams.

capabilities.intent: Discover existing songs, BGM, soundtrack cues, platform audio, or specific music versions before download.

providers.name: Web search + music source filters
providers.notes: Search for official artist/label uploads, official audio/MV, verified lyric videos, royalty-free sources, or the exact requested cover/version; record rights and attribution metadata.

providers.name: yt-dlp search extractors
providers.notes: Use ytsearch/scsearch only after the query and version constraints are clear; inspect candidates before download.

providers.name: spotdl metadata/search
providers.notes: Useful when the user names a Spotify track/playlist and metadata alignment matters; respect platform rights and user authorization.

capabilities.intent: Download or import existing music into the workspace with provenance metadata, conversion, and source-audio validation.

providers.name: yt-dlp audio download
providers.notes: General audio download/extraction path for many supported sites. For Bilibili, download raw m4a and convert with ffmpeg instead of `yt-dlp -x --audio-format mp3`; verify mean volume after conversion.

providers.name: spotdl
providers.notes: Downloads tracks/playlists with Spotify metadata and matched audio sources. Use only when the user is authorized to use the resulting music.

providers.name: scdl
providers.notes: SoundCloud-focused downloader for public/authorized SoundCloud tracks.

providers.name: bandcamp-dl
providers.notes: Bandcamp-focused downloader for public or user-owned Bandcamp tracks/albums.

providers.name: local file import + ffmpeg
providers.notes: Use when the user supplies local music files; normalize/convert only after validating the correct version and clean audio.

capabilities.intent: Record screen, webcam, or application windows to a video file.
Produce a video clip from a text prompt or reference image.

providers.name: jimeng
providers.notes: ByteDance Dreamina; shares model family with Seedance.

providers.name: Runway Gen-4
providers.notes: ~$0.05/sec, cinematic motion.

providers.name: Seedance
providers.notes: ByteDance Seedance; realistic motion.

capabilities.intent: Record, clean, trim, normalize, or denoise audio tracks.
Generate speech audio from text (narration / voiceover).
Generate background music or sound effects that follow the planned audio arc rather than a flat continuous bed.
Design hits, risers, score sections, mix dynamics, and the final audio arc as a separate pass; a music bed alone is not a designed mix.

providers.name: Agent-native sound plan
providers.notes: Read cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/sound-design.md, then write sound_design.md with stems, cue times, story function, ducking notes, and section loudness targets; shape the ending/final act intentionally (escalation, silence/hold, hit/drop, source-audio reveal, or resolve).

providers.name: ffmpeg/sox procedural stems
providers.notes: Short SFX, impacts, whooshes, and risers only; filter, envelope, and gain-stage every generated stem. Never use raw Gaussian/full-band noise as a music bed or repeated transition effect.

providers.name: pydub/numpy procedural stems
providers.notes: Programmatic stem synthesis and mixing; same procedural-audio gate as the ffmpeg/sox path — accent stems only, not the main music bed for polished 60+ second videos.

providers.name: Generated music provider
providers.notes: Locally generated score sections via the provider chosen for music.generate (suno checked here; minimax-cli or Udio with UDIO_API_KEY also apply). Follow music.generate cost/consent rules.

providers.name: Downloaded/authorized music
providers.notes: Route through music.search + music.download, or install the music-downloader skill with `npx skills add https://github.com/nymbo/skills --skill music-downloader`. Use only relevant music the user is authorized to use; run the source-audio gate before mixing source audio with new music or narration.

capabilities.intent: Segment, label, OCR, and index video footage so an editor can find usable shots across many clips.

providers.name: PySceneDetect scenedetect
providers.notes: Fast local scene cut detection; can save scene lists and start/mid/end keyframes. For found-footage edits, build scene_library.json and use cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/source-triage.md for the source rejection checklist and contact-sheet requirements.

providers.name: Google Cloud Video Intelligence
providers.notes: Use for labels, people, objects, text, speech, and shot-change annotations when cloud processing is acceptable.

providers.name: TwelveLabs video search/index
providers.notes: Use for semantic search over larger footage libraries and multimodal visual/audio indexing.

providers.name: PaddleOCR on sampled keyframes
providers.notes: Run after keyframe extraction to index visible text, signage, lower thirds, and hard subtitles; it complements scene detection rather than replacing it.

capabilities.intent: Transcribe speech to text and produce subtitle files (SRT/ASS).

providers.name: cli-anything-videocaptioner
providers.notes: Can use cloud ASR backends if configured.

providers.name: openai-whisper
providers.notes: First use may download model weights. Prefer small/base CPU models for drafts; use larger models only when runtime/resources fit the task.

providers.name: faster-whisper/stable-ts
providers.notes: Package availability does not guarantee model cache or acceptable runtime. Choose model size by quality/time constraints.

capabilities.intent: Design, time, render, and QC visible captions, subtitles, lyrics, karaoke, lower thirds, and trailer title hits so they look polished and genre-appropriate.

providers.name: Captions reference module
providers.notes: Read cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/captions.md. Use for polished visible captions, subtitles, lyrics, lower thirds, genre presets, safe-zone rules, and caption QC.

providers.name: ASS + ffmpeg subtitles
providers.notes: Deterministic local path for styled subtitle burn-in on edited footage; supply fontsdir when custom fonts are used.

providers.name: HyperFrames captions workflow
providers.notes: Install with `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes --skill hyperframes`. Use only when captions are part of an HTML/CSS/GSAP motion composition, karaoke, audio-reactive typography, or digital launch video.

providers.name: pysubs2 ASS authoring
providers.notes: Programmatically author ASS events/styles while keeping final burn-in deterministic through ffmpeg.

providers.name: MoviePy/Pillow transparent overlays
providers.notes: Use only when custom layout exceeds ASS and the project already uses a Python render path; inspect caption-heavy frames before delivery.

capabilities.intent: Assemble clips on a timeline with transitions, following the creative direction, story arc, source roles, and audio plan.

providers.name: cli-anything-kdenlive
providers.notes: Use the provider-boundary pattern in cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/nle-shotcut-kdenlive.md: ffmpeg for mezzanine prep, the NLE harness for project/timeline/tracks/transitions/render, then ffmpeg for post-NLE captions, normalization, muxing, and doctor evidence.

providers.name: cli-anything-shotcut
providers.notes: Use the provider-boundary pattern in cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/nle-shotcut-kdenlive.md: ffmpeg for mezzanine prep, the NLE harness for project/timeline/tracks/transitions/render, then ffmpeg for post-NLE captions, normalization, muxing, and doctor evidence.

providers.name: HyperFrames skill/CLI
providers.notes: Install skill with `npx skills add heygen-com/hyperframes`; CLI can also run via `npx hyperframes preview/render`. Use only for website/product-page or digital UI-driven launch videos where HTML/CSS/GSAP composition is appropriate, not as a general NLE or Remotion/React path.

capabilities.intent: Apply already-designed caption/subtitle assets, watermarks, or picture-in-picture layers onto a video.
Create a thumbnail image or social card.
Final mux / codec / container for the output video.
Check rendered video for technical, editorial, story-structure, and audio defects before delivery.

providers.name: ffmpeg/ffprobe QC filters
providers.notes: Primary local QC path for metadata, black frames, silence, freezes, crop/aspect checks, and LUFS/loudness analysis. For polished videos, also run the art gates in cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/art-direction-review.md and the story/audio rubric in references/story-structure-audio.md.

providers.name: MediaInfo CLI
providers.notes: Use for independent container/codec/bitrate/duration/aspect-ratio metadata checks.

providers.name: ffmpeg-quality-metrics / VMAF
providers.notes: Install with `pipx install ffmpeg-quality-metrics`; source: https://github.com/slhck/ffmpeg-quality-metrics. Use VMAF/SSIM/PSNR only when a reference video exists.

providers.name: Video doctor helper
providers.notes: Run cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/scripts/video_doctor.py with the system Python; subcommands probe/captions/sources/frames/tail/audio/lint produce investigation evidence, not pass/fail verdicts. For every promoted final, follow cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/render-doctor.md (hash the candidate, regenerate frames from the promoted path, refresh reports).

recipes.description: Fully-generative social short.
Record a screen walkthrough with narration and subtitles.
Webcam + b-roll + captions.
Audio-first, visualize and caption for video platforms.
Find internet clips, download them with provenance, then edit a montage.
Build a music video around a user-specified or discovered existing song.
Product/site-driven launch video with animated UI, typography, and motion graphics.

known_gaps.reason: No first-party or public CLI for YouTube/TikTok/Bilibili/Instagram yet.
known_gaps.workaround: Instruct the user to upload manually via the web UI, or escalate to a custom script using each platform's v3 API with an OAuth token the user supplies.

known_gaps.reason: Sota-tier cinematic output is only available via paid APIs (Runway, Kling, Seedance).
known_gaps.workaround: Use generate-veo-video or jimeng for high-quality output when configured; avoid pulling complex local GPU/weights stacks into the matrix by default.

known_gaps.reason: No automated license/TOS/provenance verifier for web-sourced video or music.
known_gaps.workaround: Save sources.json/music_sources.json with source URL, creator, license, intended use, and attribution text; ask the user before using unclear or restricted media. Use cli-hub-matrix/video-creation/references/source-triage.md for platform-origin evidence levels.

known_gaps.reason: External agent skills are reported as agent-installable instead of available/missing because their local install state is not machine-checkable by the env/binary/package preflight contract.
known_gaps.workaround: Use provider notes and the SKILL.md source/install table to install or inspect the external skill before relying on it.

matrices.suggest_to_user_template: To enable <capability> via <provider>, please set <ENV_VAR>. Cost: <cost_tier>. Quality: <quality_tier>. Reply 'skip' to fall back to <next provider>.

matrices.name: knowledge-research
matrices.description: Capability-based matrix for research, note-taking, document authoring, and publishing: web/literature search, article retrieval, citations/references, notes/PKM, outlining, multi-doc synthesis, office document authoring (DOCX/XLSX/PPTX/PDF), format conversion, PDF manipulation, diagrams, web publishing, and LaTeX builds. Providers include harness CLIs, public CLIs, Python libs, native binaries, and cloud APIs.

capabilities.intent: Search the web and academic literature for candidate sources on a topic.

providers.name: cli-anything-browser / safari / clibrowser
providers.notes: Browser-driven search and navigation; cli-anything-safari (macOS) and clibrowser are equivalent harness paths.

capabilities.intent: Fetch pages and extract readable article text, including JS-heavy pages.

providers.name: trafilatura
providers.notes: Best default extractor; extraction also works offline on saved HTML.

providers.name: playwright
providers.notes: Needs `playwright install` browser download on first use; best for JS-heavy pages.

capabilities.intent: Manage citations, bibliographies, BibTeX, and reference metadata.

providers.name: pyzotero
providers.notes: Needs a Zotero API key and library ID.

providers.name: Crossref REST
providers.notes: No key required; add a mailto for the polite pool.

providers.name: Semantic Scholar
providers.notes: SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY is optional for higher rate limits.

capabilities.intent: Create, search, and maintain notes in a personal knowledge management system.

providers.name: obsidian-cli
providers.notes: Official Obsidian CLI bundled with Obsidian 1.12+; install via `cli-hub install obsidian-cli` and point it at a vault.

providers.name: Notion API
providers.notes: Free tier suffices for most users; round-trips block/page IDs.

capabilities.intent: Build and manipulate hierarchical outlines (OPML/YAML/markdown) for documents and plans.
Summarize and synthesize across multiple documents or sources into briefs, digests, or reports.

providers.name: cli-anything-intelwatch
providers.notes: Partially offline; metered when cloud backends are configured.

providers.name: langchain / llama_index + local LLM
providers.notes: Pair with a local model via ollama; llama_index is an equivalent path.

providers.name: haystack
providers.notes: Partial offline support depending on pipeline components.

providers.name: Claude / OpenAI / Gemini long context
providers.notes: Any long-context model key works (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY checked here; OPENAI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY also apply).

capabilities.intent: Author office documents — DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, PDF — from structured content.

providers.name: python-docx / openpyxl / python-pptx / reportlab
providers.notes: Install per output format; import names are docx (DOCX), openpyxl (XLSX), pptx (PPTX), reportlab (PDF).

providers.name: Google Docs API
providers.notes: OAuth/service-account credentials required.

providers.name: Microsoft Graph
providers.notes: Azure app registration + token required.

capabilities.intent: Convert documents between formats (markdown, DOCX, HTML, PDF, EPUB, ...).

providers.name: docx2pdf
providers.notes: Needs Word or LibreOffice installed.

capabilities.intent: Split, merge, OCR, and extract text/tables from PDFs.

providers.name: qpdf / pdftk
providers.notes: pdftk is an equivalent alternative for split/merge/forms.

providers.name: pdfplumber
providers.notes: Best local path for text and table extraction.

providers.name: pdf2image
providers.notes: Needs poppler installed.

providers.name: Adobe PDF Services
providers.notes: Adobe PDF Services credentials (client id + secret).

capabilities.intent: Create diagrams, flowcharts, and architecture drawings.

providers.name: plantuml
providers.notes: Needs Java.

capabilities.intent: Publish articles or posts to a website, blog, or CMS.

providers.name: contentful
providers.notes: Needs a Contentful space token; install via `cli-hub install contentful`.

providers.name: sanity
providers.notes: Needs a Sanity project token; install via `cli-hub install sanity`.

providers.name: hugo / mkdocs / jekyll / pelican
providers.notes: Static-site path (hugo checked here); mkdocs/pelican are Python equivalents, jekyll is Ruby.

providers.name: WordPress REST
providers.notes: Application password/token for the target site.

providers.name: Medium (legacy)
providers.notes: Legacy API; availability not guaranteed.

capabilities.intent: Build PDFs from LaTeX sources.

providers.name: pylatex
providers.notes: Needs a TeX distribution installed.

recipes.description: Survey a topic, extract key papers, synthesize.
Turn a recorded meeting into a structured document.
recipes.notes: Transcription comes from the video-creation (S1) matrix's text.transcribe capability; run it there first.

recipes.description: Research, write, and publish a post.
Research + citations + LaTeX build.
Fetch competitor pages, extract positioning, produce a briefing deck (PPTX).
Ingest a PDF book, OCR if needed, produce chapter summaries + quote file.

known_gaps.reason: Notion API exists as an escalation, but there is no harness for local-first Notion workflows (recurs in S11 team comms).
known_gaps.workaround: Use cli-anything-obsidian or raw markdown locally, then sync to Notion via the API when NOTION_API_KEY is set.

known_gaps.reason: Google Docs / Microsoft 365 live editing is API-path only.
known_gaps.workaround: Author locally (LibreOffice / python-docx) and upload, or escalate to Google Docs API / Microsoft Graph with user-supplied credentials.

known_gaps.reason: No agent-native LaTeX harness; latexmk + Tectonic cover the native path.
known_gaps.workaround: Use the latexmk/Tectonic native binaries or pylatex.

known_gaps.reason: No first-party translation CLI; Python fallbacks and APIs only.
known_gaps.workaround: Translate directly as agent work, or escalate to a translation API the user supplies.

matrices.suggest_to_user_template: To enable <capability> via <provider>, please set <ENV_VAR>. Cost: <cost_tier>. Quality: <quality_tier>. Reply 'skip' to fall back to <next provider>.

matrices.name: 3d-cad
matrices.description: Capability-based matrix for 3D modeling, parametric CAD, sculpting, point clouds, photogrammetry, PBR texturing, preview/offline rendering, GPU frame debugging, fabrication (slicing and CAM), and game-engine export. Mostly offline-capable; providers include harness CLIs, Python libs, native binaries, and cloud APIs.

capabilities.intent: Create, edit, and repair polygon meshes (organic or scanned geometry).

providers.name: pygalmesh
providers.notes: Needs CGAL installed.

capabilities.intent: Model dimension-driven parts with parametric CAD.

providers.name: SolidPython
providers.notes: Generates OpenSCAD code; needs the openscad binary to render.

capabilities.intent: Sculpt organic forms digitally.

providers.name: cli-anything-blender (sculpt mode, scripted)
providers.notes: Agent-driven sculpting is impractical beyond Blender scripts; see known gaps (no ZBrush/Nomad path).

capabilities.intent: Ingest, clean, register, and convert point clouds.
Reconstruct 3D meshes from photo sets.

providers.name: RealityCapture
providers.notes: Commercial license required.

capabilities.intent: Create PBR textures and materials for meshes.
Produce fast viewport/preview renders.
Produce final path-traced renders.

providers.name: cli-anything-blender (Cycles)
providers.notes: GPU strongly recommended for acceptable render times.

capabilities.intent: Capture and inspect GPU frames for graphics debugging.
Slice meshes into 3D-printer gcode.

providers.name: PrusaSlicer CLI
providers.notes: Check the mesh is manifold first (trimesh.Trimesh.is_volume or pymeshlab); slicers fail silently on non-manifold meshes.

capabilities.intent: Generate CNC/CAM toolpaths from CAD models.

providers.name: kiri:moto
providers.notes: Grid.Space kiri:moto; also runs as a local web app.

providers.name: Fusion 360 CAM / Onshape CAM
providers.notes: Onshape CAM via ONSHAPE_ACCESS_KEY is the equivalent escalation.

capabilities.intent: Export/convert assets (glTF/FBX) and hand off to a game or viz engine.

providers.name: cli-anything-blender (glTF/FBX export)
providers.notes: Apply modifiers and bake materials before export; runtime engines don't read Blender shader graphs.

recipes.description: Parametric CAD, then mesh, slice, and gcode.
Model, texture, and export to an engine.
Photos to photogrammetry, clean mesh, then export.
Parametric part, then CAM toolpath and gcode.
CAD import, scene setup, offline render.
Capture a GPU frame, analyze, patch shader.

known_gaps.reason: No open agent-native path approaches Substance 3D quality; Blender shader nodes are the current ceiling.
known_gaps.workaround: Use scripted Blender shader nodes or bpy/Pillow procedural maps; ask the user for Substance-authored textures when quality is critical.

known_gaps.reason: PrusaSlicer/CuraEngine natives work but there is no harness-level wrapper (Bambu Studio / OrcaSlicer uncovered).
known_gaps.workaround: Drive the native slicer CLIs directly; verify the mesh is manifold first.

known_gaps.reason: Agent-driven sculpting is impractical beyond Blender scripts; no ZBrush/Nomad path.
known_gaps.workaround: Use Blender sculpt-mode scripting for coarse forms or ask the user to sculpt manually.

known_gaps.reason: Only Godot is covered on the harness side; no Unity Asset Pipeline / Unreal Datasmith path.
known_gaps.workaround: Export glTF/FBX and instruct the user to import into Unity/Unreal manually.

matrices.suggest_to_user_template: To enable <capability> via <provider>, please set <ENV_VAR>. Cost: <cost_tier>. Quality: <quality_tier>. Reply 'skip' to fall back to <next provider>.

matrices.name: game-development
matrices.description: Capability-based matrix for game development: engine authoring (Godot-first), 3D/2D/audio/notation assets, AI-generated assets, agent-driven playtesting, build packaging, store publishing, and telemetry/crash reporting. Providers include harness CLIs, public CLIs, Python libs, native binaries, and cloud APIs.

capabilities.intent: Author and manipulate game projects, scenes, and scripts in an engine.
Produce 3D game assets (modeling, baking, engine-ready export).

providers.name: cli-anything-blender
providers.notes: The full pipeline lives in the 3d-cad (S3) matrix: model.mesh, material.texture, export.engine.

capabilities.intent: Create sprites, tiles, and UI art.

providers.name: aseprite
providers.notes: Commercial license required.

capabilities.intent: Create and process SFX and gameplay audio.
Compose and export music notation / scores (MIDI, MusicXML).
Generate art/audio assets for games with AI, with reproducible seeds and prompts.

providers.name: cli-anything-comfyui
providers.notes: Needs ComfyUI plus model weights.

providers.name: jimeng
providers.notes: ByteDance Dreamina; install via `cli-hub install jimeng`.

providers.name: diffusers
providers.notes: Needs model weights and ideally a GPU.

capabilities.intent: Drive a live game loop or test harness with an agent.

providers.name: cli-anything-slay-the-spire-ii
providers.notes: Requires Slay the Spire II plus the bridge mod; ask the user if modding is permitted before attempting.

providers.name: Godot headless + scripted input
providers.notes: Run Godot with --headless and GUT or scripted input.

capabilities.intent: Package a game build/binary for distribution.

providers.name: cli-anything-godot (export presets)
providers.notes: Needs Godot export templates installed. Version builds into builds/<version>/ with a manifest.

capabilities.intent: Upload a build to a game store; always gated behind explicit user confirmation.

providers.name: steamcmd
providers.notes: One-time $100 Steamworks fee and an app ID; use a dedicated build account to limit credential risk.

providers.name: Google Play Developer
providers.notes: Service account required.

capabilities.intent: Collect gameplay analytics and crash reports.

providers.name: sentry
providers.notes: Free tier with paid scale; install via `cli-hub install sentry`.

providers.name: posthog
providers.notes: Free tier with paid scale.

providers.name: Unity Analytics
providers.notes: Unity project token required.

recipes.description: Minimal end-to-end for a game jam.
recipes.notes: publish.store is optional and always needs explicit user confirmation.

recipes.description: Train / evaluate an agent against an existing game.
AI-generated art plus a simple engine loop.
Ship to Steam with crash reporting.
Composed score integrated with gameplay.
Photogrammetry asset, import, ship.
recipes.notes: Photogrammetry reconstruction comes from the 3d-cad (S3) matrix's photogrammetry.reconstruct capability.

known_gaps.reason: Steam/itch/console wrappers are native or API only; no harness-level abstraction.
known_gaps.workaround: Escalate via the suggest-to-user template with platform-specific credentials; never auto-publish without explicit user confirmation.

known_gaps.reason: Only Godot has a harness; no Unity/Unreal path.
known_gaps.workaround: Start in Godot unless the user explicitly requires Unity/Unreal; for those, fall back to user-driven editor work.

known_gaps.reason: Sentry works via the public CLI; no first-party path for GameAnalytics/PlayFab.
known_gaps.workaround: Use the sentry public CLI, or API escalation with user-supplied keys.

matrices.suggest_to_user_template: To enable <capability> via <provider>, please set <ENV_VAR>. Cost: <cost_tier>. Quality: <quality_tier>. Reply 'skip' to fall back to <next provider>.

matrices.name: image-design
matrices.description: Capability-based matrix for image and graphic design: AI image generation, raster/vector editing, UI mockups/wireframes, diagrams, super-resolution upscaling, photo library management, RAW development, and CMS publishing. GIMP/Krita/Inkscape/ComfyUI cover most work offline; cloud APIs escalate quality.

capabilities.intent: Produce an image from a text prompt or reference image.

providers.name: cli-anything-comfyui
providers.notes: Needs ComfyUI plus model weights (FLUX/SDXL).

providers.name: jimeng
providers.notes: ByteDance Dreamina; install via `cli-hub install jimeng`.

providers.name: diffusers (SD/SDXL/FLUX)
providers.notes: Needs model weights and ideally a GPU.

providers.name: OpenAI GPT-Image-1
providers.notes: Best for photoreal and text-in-image.

providers.name: Ideogram
providers.notes: Strong poster/typography rendering.

providers.name: Midjourney (Discord bridge)
providers.notes: Discord bridge only; no official API.

capabilities.intent: Edit raster images: retouch, composite, resize, background removal.
Create and edit vector graphics (SVG): logos, icons, illustrations.

providers.name: cairosvg
providers.notes: Also the batch rasterization path for icon/logo exports.

capabilities.intent: Design UI mockups and wireframes.

providers.name: cli-anything-sketch
providers.notes: Sketch is a paid macOS app.

providers.name: penpot
providers.notes: Self-hostable.

providers.name: Figma API
providers.notes: Free tier works for most users; design-system aware.

capabilities.intent: Create diagrams and flowcharts.

providers.notes: Face restoration; needs model weights.

capabilities.intent: Organize, tag, and query a photo library (DAM).

providers.notes: OAuth credentials required; free tier.

providers.notes: Adobe credentials required.

capabilities.intent: Publish images or articles to a CMS or static site.

providers.name: contentful
providers.notes: Needs a Contentful space token; install via `cli-hub install contentful`.

providers.name: sanity
providers.notes: Needs a Sanity project token; install via `cli-hub install sanity`.

providers.name: hugo / mkdocs
providers.notes: Static-site path (hugo checked here); mkdocs is the Python equivalent.

providers.name: WordPress REST
providers.notes: Application password/token for the target site.

known_gaps.reason: Figma is the headline cross-scenario gap (S5 and S7); API escalation only, no native harness.
known_gaps.workaround: Use the Figma API with FIGMA_TOKEN, or fall back to cli-anything-sketch / drawio wireframes.

known_gaps.reason: Strong native CLIs (exiftool, digikam, photoprism) and equally strong photo.develop natives, but no harness.
Midjourney has no clean API; Discord bridge only.
known_gaps.workaround: Prefer GPT-Image-1 / Ideogram / Stability / Recraft when credentialed, or local ComfyUI / diffusers.

matrices.suggest_to_user_template: To enable <capability> via <provider>, please set <ENV_VAR>. Cost: <cost_tier>. Quality: <quality_tier>. Reply 'skip' to fall back to <next provider>.
